strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of New Hampshire is a public land-grant, R1 research university known for its experiential learning, accessibility, and high career placement rates. Serving as the flagship institution of the state, it offers a traditional New England campus experience with deep ties to the regional Boston workforce.

analyst_takeaways.insight: High overall acceptance rate masks strict internal selectivity for restricted majors like Nursing and Exercise Science.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants to health sciences must apply directly from high school with elite credentials, while general admits have an accessible entry path.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The Granite Guarantee covers full tuition for Pell-eligible in-state residents, but out-of-state COA approaches $60,000.
analyst_takeaways.implication: UNH is an unparalleled value for lower-income NH residents, but out-of-state students must aggressively pursue merit aid to optimize ROI.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Designation as an R1 institution provides rare access to high-level research for undergraduates.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students in STEM and Business (via the FIRE program) can build highly competitive, experience-based resumes prior to graduation.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A traditional, outdoors-oriented New England college town setting located 15 minutes from the coast and an hour from the White Mountains, featuring Division I sports and over 200 student clubs.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The Amtrak Downeaster train stops directly on campus, providing seamless and vital connectivity to Boston and Portland for internships and travel.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Over 1,800 undergraduates participate in the annual Undergraduate Research Conference, benefiting directly from UNH's partnerships with NASA and NIH.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test optional. Standardized test scores are considered if submitted, but not required for admission or most scholarships.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed in recent Common Data Sets.

gpa.notes: GPA is recalculated by admissions using academic core courses on a 4.0 scale.

colleges.name: College of Health and Human Services (CHHS)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with top-tier STEM grades seeking clinical healthcare careers.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Strict clinical capacity limits; must maintain a cumulative 2.5 GPA throughout the program.
majors.notes: Internal transfer is effectively closed or extremely rare due to cohort size limits.

majors.name: Occupational Therapy
majors.special_requirements: Kept small to meet accreditation requirements for student:faculty ratios.
majors.notes: Highly restricted; internal transfers admitted only as space allows.

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Physical Sciences (CEPS)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Analytically minded students seeking robust research and lab opportunities.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Must achieve C- or better in core CS courses to remain in the major. Only two repeats of core courses are allowed before dismissal.
majors.notes: B.A. option also available for students looking to double major.

colleges.name: Peter T. Paul College of Business and Economics
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Aspiring corporate leaders and entrepreneurs wanting experiential learning.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Must declare an option by the second semester of sophomore year.
majors.notes: Features the FIRE (First-Year Innovation and Research Experience) program.

supplemental_requirements.details: No supplemental essays or writing prompts are required other than the standard Common Application personal statement.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: UNH utilizes an 'Alternative Major' pathway, offering admission to a secondary major or an undeclared program if an applicant does not meet the criteria for a competitive first-choice major.

major_selection_tactics: Apply to impacted majors (Nursing, OT, Exercise Science) directly out of high school, as internal transfer spaces are highly limited or closed.
Utilize 'COLA Undeclared' or 'CHHS Undeclared' as a safety net if your STEM grades are borderline for competitive majors.
Leverage the Early Action deadline for a massive statistical advantage (historical ~90% admit rate) and scholarship priority.
college_ranking_tactics: Capitalize on UNH's test-optional policy by withholding sub-1090 SAT scores to allow the holistic review to focus entirely on transcript trends.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UNH frequently admits students who miss the mark for competitive majors into their second-choice major or into an undeclared track. Internal transfer is possible post-enrollment by achieving high foundational course grades.

scholarships.name: Granite Guarantee
scholarships.benefits: Covers the absolute full cost of tuition for any Pell Grant-eligible New Hampshire resident.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Annual tuition offset for high-achieving resident and non-resident students.

ap_policy.general_rule: Credits generally awarded for scores of 3, 4, or 5 depending on the department.
ap_policy.usage: Used to bypass introductory courses or fulfill Discovery Program general education requirements.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Most general education requirements are waived for NH community college students arriving with an Associate's degree, provided they earned a C- or better in their courses.

what_it_takes: A weighted GPA around 3.63, with a transcript reflecting mostly A's and B's.
Completion of core subjects (English, Math, Science, Social Studies, World Language) all four years.
For health majors, high grades in preparatory sciences like Anatomy and Physiology.
essay_tips: Since there are no supplemental essays, your Common App personal statement must carry the full weight of demonstrating your character and community fit.
red_flags: Downward academic trends during junior or senior year.
Applying to Health Sciences without foundational science courses on the transcript.
GPA below 2.8 overall, or below 3.0 for competitive business/science majors.
insights: Students praise the picturesque setting and proximity to the ocean and mountains, as well as the Amtrak Downeaster providing an easy escape to Boston.
The Career and Professional Success (CaPS) office is highly engaged, driving a 94%+ employment/grad school placement rate.
